- Generated file marker: `<!-- Generated by Agent Builder Kit -->` MUST be placed on the first line AFTER the frontmatter closing `---`, never before the opening `---` (placing it before frontmatter breaks YAML parsing)
- `agents` (only when subagents are needed): MUST use inline array with exact display names
  - Correct: `agents: ["Exact Agent Display Name 1", "Exact Agent Display Name 2"]`
  - Wrong: `agents:\n  - some-agent.agent` (block list with filenames)
  - Wrong: `agents:\n  - Some Agent Name` (block list even with display names)
- `handoffs` (when agent delegates work): array of {agent, label, prompt} objects
  - `agent`: exact display name (same as used in `agents` field)
  - `label`: short action text shown as UI button
  - `prompt`: contextual delegation prompt for the target agent
- Do not include `tools` or `mcp-servers` unless explicitly requested
- `description` must be a double-quoted string with trigger phrases
-->

## Operating Model

### 1. Clarify
- <Questions or criteria>

- <Never write questions as plain response text — `vscode_askQuestions` keeps the session alive>

## Collaboration Model

### Inputs From Other Agents Or User
- <What this agent expects to receive before it starts>

### Handoffs To Other Agents
- <What this agent passes downstream and to whom>
- <Each handoff must be declared in the `handoffs:` frontmatter field>

### Transition Criteria
- Pass: <What must be true for this agent to hand work forward>
- Revise: <What should cause this agent to send work back to an upstream or peer specialist>
- Blocked: <What should stop automatic workflow progress and require escalation>

### Return Or Escalation Conditions
- Auto-return: <When this agent should send work back for revision without asking the user first>
- User escalation: <When this agent should escalate to the user instead of continuing automatically>

## Evidence Standard

- Every business-rule claim must be backed by code/doc evidence, user confirmation, or labeled:
  - `[ASSUMPTION]` — reasonable inference, not yet verified
  - `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]` — ambiguous, requires user input or further investigation
- Resolve all `[ASSUMPTION]` labels before passing work to downstream agents
- Challenge upstream `[ASSUMPTION]` labels that arrive unresolved

## Decision Rules

- <Rule 1>
- <Rule 2>

## Tool Usage

- Use all available tools including MCP servers configured by the user
- When MCP servers are configured for external services (issue trackers, project management, CI/CD, documentation), prefer MCP tools over URL fetching
- Do not attempt to fetch URLs directly for services that require authentication — use the appropriate MCP tool instead
- Use `vscode_askQuestions` for all structured clarification — never write questions as plain response text
- Use `manage_todo_list` for tracking progress on multi-step tasks
- Use session memory (`/memories/session/`) to persist investigation findings, plans, and progress for complex tasks

## Context Management

- For complex tasks (>10 files, >3 modules, migration scope): persist plans and progress to session memory
- After each major phase: update progress notes, compact context if conversation is growing large
- Read existing session memory notes at the start of work to re-ground context
- Keep inter-agent outputs structured (tables, lists, file references) for agent legibility
